response: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) consistently ranks #1 among universities, enrolling roughly 11,500 students with an extremely low acceptance rate of 3.9 percent. The institution charges $57,590 per year in tuition, supports a $24.6 billion endowment, and employs 1,069 faculty members who have produced 98 Nobel laureates. Founded in 1861, MIT remains a leading hub for science, engineering, and innovation.
